concept: Member / Recognized Participant in a Group or Institution
description: Recognized participation in a group under a stated criterion and source. A person can belong to a place ('wema') without carrying this institutional status, and taking part in one event does not by itself make anyone a member.
sound_symbolism: 'We.ma' repeats the full sound of 'wema' (belong): a rounded glide into the stressed closure and broad release of 'ma'. 'Ri' adds a quick returning contact of the tongue and brightens the end. Belonging is heard first; membership gives that relation one further mark in the shared record.
grammatical_notes: A countable noun commonly modified by the group or institution: 'ha sila wemari mua kowela pawela.' (This community member participates in the council.) State the membership source, criteria, time, and disputed status where relevant. Distinct from 'wema' (belong), 'pawela' (participate), residence, affected standing, and 'shalori' (represent).

pillars.solarpunk-values: Membership can make shared stewardship and decision participation visible while keeping entry, exclusion, burden, and institutional power open to criticism.
pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: Naming membership separately from agreement, consent, representation, and worth prevents a group label from speaking over the person who bears it.

tags.community: recognized membership in a stated group or institution
